segments.short_label: Comprehensive Investigation: Urban-Rural Political Polarization in Bern Elections
segments.summary: Extensive investigative report on political and social polarization between urban Bern and rural areas during cantonal elections. Documents SVP politician Daniel Bichsel campaigning in rural areas and Green politician Aline Trede in the countryside. Includes analysis by political geographer Michael Hermann on increasing polarization driven by residential sorting. Shows contrasting campaign events, voter reactions, and election results with Trede defending her green government seat while Bichsel lost due to lack of urban support. Overall government remains in conservative hands with four bourgeois and three left seats.

segments.short_label: Investigation: Iranian Regime Supporters in Switzerland and Universities
segments.summary: Extensive investigative report into supporters of the Iranian Islamic Republic in Switzerland. Features Imam Seyyed Behbahani in Geneva leading Shia Muslim community at Ahl-El-Bait foundation. Reveals connections between Swiss-based Islamic centers and Iranian state influence. Documents Mahmood Khalilzadeh, president of Islamic center in Schlieren, who previously led center in Frankfurt closed by German authorities for ties to Iranian extremism. Investigates Iranian students and researchers at EPFL with connections to Iranian regime, including cases with Basij militia background. Documents vulnerable positions at Swiss universities and difficulty distinguishing regime supporters from opposition students.
segments.segment_text: Er gehört zur Minderheit der Regimetreuen innerhalb der iranischen Diaspora. Und er ist der einzige, der mit unserem Reporter vor der Kamera sprechen wollte. segments.short_label: Investigation: Swiss-Based Livestreamed Child Sexual Abuse on Philippines
segments.summary: Extensive investigative report on a man from canton Vaud (pseudonym 'Christian') who paid families in the Philippines to sexually abuse children and streamed the abuse live via video calls. Documents the case through 11,000 chat messages and video calls from police records. Man convicted in 2025 to 10 years prison after physically abusing a 4-year-old in Switzerland. Journalists traveled to Philippines to find victims and investigate streaming infrastructure, interviewing police, victim protection organizations, and Preda Foundation. Identifies three named victims - Lina, Jason, and Endrick - who recount traumatic experiences. Documents approximately 30 total victims and mentions related case of Aargau resident convicted of child pornography. Philippine police lacked cooperation with Swiss authorities. Reveals economic incentives for families and infrastructure enabling abuse.
